evidence.scientific_clarity: Three methods with mean lines and 95% CI bands on log-scale y-axis, gap annotated with double-headed arrow and delta value — fully matches brief.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVu Sans — the matplotlib default, not a professional font choice.
evidence.palette: Uses Wong colorblind-safe palette (#555555 gray, #0072B2 blue, #D55E00 orange); no green/red reserved for directional meaning.
evidence.layout_density: Compact 4.5x3.2 in figure, tight_layout(), xlim clipped to data range, log scale fills vertical space, top/right spines removed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content; legend frameless at upper right; gap annotated with delta; n=5 seeds stated; no obvious Reviewer 2 targets.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows BMQQDV+DejaVuSans as Type 3 (not Type 42) — text is bitmapped, not selectable; pdf.fonttype=42 was never set in the script.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (17K), figure.png (131K), figure.svg (36K), caption.tex (360B), gen_figure.py (2.0K).
evidence.caption_quality: Bold title, explains lines and shaded bands, cites n=5 seeds, states directional finding, and gives specific numerical claim (delta=0.15).

overall_strengths: Colorblind-safe Wong palette applied correctly with no green/red directional confusion.
Caption is publication-ready: bold title, methodology detail (n=5 seeds), and a concrete numerical finding (delta=0.15).
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none') means all text in the PDF/SVG is bitmapped Type 3 — not selectable, not editable by journal production.
Default DejaVu Sans font used throughout; a Nature/Science-style figure should specify a professional serif or sans-serif (e.g. Helvetica, Arial, or a custom rcParams font family).
